cost_sensitivity.cost_model: Future diagnostics must reject interpretation if any roll-window readout only survives before fees, slippage, and spread assumptions.
cost_sensitivity.scope: Value-free migration metadata only; no execution cost estimate is produced.

cost_assumptions.scope: research planning only; no execution or tradability claim

data_assumptions.data_dependency_detail: Quarterly roll dates are deterministic from the existing CME_EQUITY_INDEX_QUARTERLY_ROLL_POLICY_ID roll calendar already used by the roll guard; no external feed required. The roll-window classification logic already exists labels-side and is reused, not rebuilt.
data_assumptions.missing_label_candidates: No new label candidates are declared by this legacy card.

exclusion_rules: Condition only on a known-ahead, backward-looking roll-window membership flag derived from the existing quarterly roll calendar (not the offline countdown features). Critically, the OUTCOME labels must be passed through the existing evaluate_roll_guard (RollCrossPolicy.DROP default, with TRUNCATE/FLAG options and a safe DROP default when the calendar is missing or ambiguous) so any forward window that crosses the roll splice is dropped/truncated rather than silently splicing two contracts.
Do not test needs_paid_data records in V0; paid-data sourcing is RED-deferred.
Do not edit or delete the legacy Track-A card until a later retirement phase is authorized.
expected_failure_modes: Roll-window membership is a deterministic calendar position derived from the quarterly roll policy, structurally independent of the realized-price/quote main effects the dead mechanisms conditioned on. Residual non-orthogonality risk is redundancy with the existing expiration-proximity / session-position features rather than with the dead price-action factors; this must be checked.

Residualize forward outcomes against the dead-mechanism factors (regime/vwap_session/bbo_tradability/liquidity_pa) before scoring the roll-window conditioner. Separately confirm the roll-window flag is not merely a proxy for the existing minutes_to_expiration feature or for the month-end / opex calendar flags from the other cards (the quarterly roll window can be temporally adjacent to quad-witching).

Quarterly roll dates are deterministic from the existing CME_EQUITY_INDEX_QUARTERLY_ROLL_POLICY_ID roll calendar already used by the roll guard; no external feed required. The roll-window classification logic already exists labels-side and is reused, not rebuilt.
promotion_criteria: Any future WATCH or CANDIDATE routing requires reviewer-gated evidence and must preserve EXPLORATORY promotion guards.
Any future diagnostic must pass no-lookahead, duplicate-exposure, and testability checks before interpretation.

timestamp_assumptions.lookahead_description: Two hazards: (1) the existing bars_to_roll / minutes_to_roll session features are explicitly OFFLINE-ONLY future-window countdowns (live=False) and must NOT be repurposed as live conditioners; (2) the roll DATE is calendar-derivable and known-ahead, but a forward outcome label entered inside the roll window can have its horizon SPAN the roll splice, which is a known leakage path.
timestamp_assumptions.lookahead_level: medium lookahead risk declared by the legacy Track-A card
timestamp_assumptions.post_event_knowable_features: The legacy card declares no post-event knowable feature list.

hypothesis_card.author: ALPHA_IDEA_TO_VERDICT_LOOP_V0/IVL-P01
hypothesis_card.expected_mechanism: During the known-ahead quarterly futures roll window (the days surrounding the front-to-next contract roll), we test whether intraday forward behavior conditioned on roll-window membership and proximity-to-roll differs from non-roll sessions. This is a falsifiable calendar-flow conditioning hypothesis, not a profitability claim.
falsification_criteria: Reject or requeue this migrated record if required governed features or labels are unavailable before diagnostics.
Block any later verdict if no-lookahead controls or duplicate exposure checks fail before a metric is inspected.
hypothesis_card.rationale: Calendar spread trading and the migration of open interest from the expiring to the deferred contract concentrate predictable flow into the roll window; whether this produces a measurable conditional forward pattern in the continuous front-contract series is an empirical question the diagnostics resolve.
risks: Two hazards: (1) the existing bars_to_roll / minutes_to_roll session features are explicitly OFFLINE-ONLY future-window countdowns (live=False) and must NOT be repurposed as live conditioners; (2) the roll DATE is calendar-derivable and known-ahead, but a forward outcome label entered inside the roll window can have its horizon SPAN the roll splice, which is a known leakage path.

Roll-window membership is a deterministic calendar position derived from the quarterly roll policy, structurally independent of the realized-price/quote main effects the dead mechanisms conditioned on. Residual non-orthogonality risk is redundancy with the existing expiration-proximity / session-position features rather than with the dead price-action factors; this must be checked.

Quarterly roll dates are deterministic from the existing CME_EQUITY_INDEX_QUARTERLY_ROLL_POLICY_ID roll calendar already used by the roll guard; no external feed required. The roll-window classification logic already exists labels-side and is reused, not rebuilt.
hypothesis_card.title: Track-A migrated main-effect hypothesis: roll_week_flow
